children.name: Anseranas
children.description: A monotypic genus representing an ancient lineage of waterfowl with a partially fused webbing and a distinctive knob on the crown. It is the sole surviving member of its family and retains several primitive anatomical features.

children.name: Anseranas semipalmata
children.description: A striking black-and-white waterfowl endemic to northern Australia and southern New Guinea, named for its pied plumage and goose-like appearance. It is unique among waterfowl in having a partly webbed foot and a slow moult that allows it to remain flightless for only a short period. It forms large, noisy flocks that graze on sedges and grasses in tropical floodplains.
